question.text: A bull put spread with 50 lots: Sell 84.00 put at 0.10, Buy 83.50 put at 0.03. USDINR at expiry = 84.50. Total P&L?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Both puts are OTM (84.50 > 84.00 > 83.50). Net premium received = 0.10 - 0.03 = 0.07 per unit. Total = 50 x 1000 x 0.07 = Rs. 3,500. This is the maximum profit for a bull put spread. **The Trap:** Rs. 5,000 uses only the sold put premium (0.10 x 50 x 1000). Must deduct the bought put cost. **Cross-Exam Context:** Bull put spread max profit scenario tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Bull put spread profits when underlying stays above the higher strike. Max profit = net premium received.
